About: It is about the ruling family of the Franks from around the middle of the 5th century until Pepin the Short in 751.
Affects: It affects the succession by being tonsured and sent to a monastery.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Roman army of northern Gaul.
CausedBy: It is caused by the breakup of the empire of Theodoric the Great.
ConsistsOf: It consists of Austrasia, Neustria, Burgundy and Aquitaine.
CreatedAround: It was created around the middle of the 5th century.
HasA: It has a distinct name stock.
It has the largest and most powerful of the states of western Europe following the breakup of the empire of Theodoric the Great.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by long hair.
It is characterized by a distinct name stock.
Influenced: It influenced the succession by being tonsured and sent to a monastery.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the concessions made by the aristocracy.
IsA: It is the ruling family of the Franks.
KnownAs: It is known as the 'long-haired kings'.
KnownFor: It is known for its long hair.
It is known for its distinct name stock.
LocatedIn: It is located in northern Gaul.
It is located in western Europe.
OccurredAt: It occurred at the Battle of Vouillé in 507.
It occurred at the Battle of Tolbiac in 496.
It occurred at the Battle of Tertry in 687.
It occurred at the Battle of Tours in 732.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Franco-Roman War of 486.
It occurred during the reign of Clotaire II.
It occurred during the reign of Dagobert I.
It occurred during the reign of Pepin the Middle.
It occurred during the reign of Charles Martel.
It occurred during the reign of Pepin the Short.

OccurredAround: It occurred around the middle of the 5th century.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the Roman army of northern Gaul.
PartOf: It is part of the Franks.
UsedAs: It is used as a ceremonial role.
It is used as a patrimony.
UsedFor: It is used for ruling the Franks.
It is used for uniting all the Franks and northern Gallo-Romans.
It is used for conquering Gaul.
It is used for extending rule into Raetia.
It is used for accepting lordship in Germania.
It is used for dividing the kingdom.
It is used for political conflicts.
